evidence.snippet: On April 6, 1962 the Synod of
evidence.reason: the passage does not state that the diocese is currently a titular see of the Patriarchate of Constantinople
evidence.window: On April 6, 1962 the Synod of the Patriarchate elected Gerasimos as titular Bishop of Abydos. On May 20, 1962 he was consecrated in Boston and would serve as the Bishop of the Diocese of Boston. At this point we must emphasize that even when he became a bishop in 1962, he believed steadfastly that the priesthood is one. Seeker of the truth, teacher of the truth and servant of the truth was the Bishop of Abydos, Gerasimos of blessed memory. When someone understands the absolute nature of this indifference, he also understand how and why Bishop Gerasimos of Abydos remained a monk of the Holy Mountain, and particularly of the Skete of St. Anna, while he lived and served in the great metropolitan cities of the two continents and in the great university centers of the world: Boston, Oxford, Munich, Athens. At times of inner difficulties all could find refuge and spiritual help in the ascetic cell of Abba Gerasimos, Bishop of Abydos. He believed that the bishop has nothing more than the priest, being only an archpriest, the first among priests. Primarily the bishop has the particular charisma from God to ordain other priests.

text_claims.text: It began digital publishing in 2008 and became Western Canada's largest online-only publication by 2016.

evidence.sentence: Childhood friends Karm Sumal and Manny Bahia launched Vancity Buzz in a South Vancouver basement in 2008.
evidence.snippet: Childhood friends Karm Sumal and Manny Bahia
evidence.reason: The passage does not state that the publication became Western Canada's largest online-only publication by 2016.
evidence.window: Childhood friends Karm Sumal and Manny Bahia launched Vancity Buzz in a South Vancouver basement in 2008. Eight years after its launch, it boasts 7.5 million monthly page views and a devout social media following that includes more than 304,000 fans on Facebook and another 153,000 Twitter followers. The site began as an information source for local events (its initial tagline was "Your inside source for Vancouver happenings") before eventually expanding into breaking news. The company's Vancouver success led the partners to launch a Calgary version, Calgary Buzz, in 2015.

spans.text: Lin Fei-fan and Chen Wei-ting, the two main leaders of a recent student-led occupation of the Legislature, established a new organization on Sunday with the primary aim of pushing for reform of the country's referendum law. Specifically, the new group, called Taiwan March, will advocate lowering the voter turnout threshold for a national referendum, which is currently 50 percent. To this end, it will launch a petition seeking at least 100,000 signatures within six months, for an amendment to the Referendum Act, Lin said at a press conference. In its broader efforts to push for political reforms, Taiwan March will collaborate with other civic groups to discuss draft bills on public issues such as free economic pilot zones in Taiwan and oversight of cross-strait agreements, Lin said. The discussions will also include legislative review of a trade-in-services pact with China, he said, referring to the issue that spurred the student-led protests in Taiwan in March.

text_claims.text: The film had an early screening at Disney's Hollywood Studios on June 8, 2019.

evidence.sentence: Activities culminated in a special surprise following a screening of the film when filmmakers Josh Cooley, Mark Nielsen and Jonas Rivera, and voice cast members Tom Hanks (voice of Woody), Tim Allen (voice of Buzz Lightyear), Annie Potts (voice of Bo Peep), Tony Hale (voice of Forky), Keegan-Michael Key (voice of Ducky) and Keanu Reeves (voice of Duke Caboom) showed up in the theater to greet guests.

evidence.window: ORLANDO, FLORIDA – JUNE 08: Tom Hanks and Tim Allen visit Toy Story Land at Disney's Hollywood Studios on June 08, 2019 in Orlando, Florida. Activities culminated in a special surprise following a screening of the film when filmmakers Josh Cooley, Mark Nielsen and Jonas Rivera, and voice cast members Tom Hanks (voice of Woody), Tim Allen (voice of Buzz Lightyear), Annie Potts (voice of Bo Peep), Tony Hale (voice of Forky), Keegan-Michael Key (voice of Ducky) and Keanu Reeves (voice of Duke Caboom) showed up in the theater to greet guests. Check out some more photos below of the cast meeting with their favorite Toy Story characters!

spans.text: ORLANDO, FLORIDA – JUNE 08: Tom Hanks, Annie Potts, Tim Allen, Tony Hale, Christina Hendricks and Keanu Reeves visit Toy Story Land at Disney's Hollywood Studios on June 08, 2019 in Orlando, Florida. Filmmakers, voice talent and walk-around characters from Disney and Pixar's "Toy Story 4" descended upon Toy Story Land at Disney's Hollywood Studios in Orlando, Florida, to celebrate the upcoming release of the movie. ORLANDO, FLORIDA – JUNE 08: Tom Hanks and Tim Allen visit Toy Story Land at Disney's Hollywood Studios on June 08, 2019 in Orlando, Florida. Activities culminated in a special surprise following a screening of the film when filmmakers Josh Cooley, Mark Nielsen and Jonas Rivera, and voice cast members Tom Hanks (voice of Woody), Tim Allen (voice of Buzz Lightyear), Annie Potts (voice of Bo Peep), Tony Hale (voice of Forky), Keegan-Michael Key (voice of Ducky) and Keanu Reeves (voice of Duke Caboom) showed up in the theater to greet guests. Check out some more photos below of the cast meeting with their favorite Toy Story characters! "Toy Story 4" opens in U.S. theaters on June 21, 2019. text_claims.text: After the game was released in Europe, Nihilistic made demos of the game available for downloading over Xbox Live and PlayStation Network.

evidence.window: THQ today announced that a new playable demo of its upcoming action title Conan has gone live on the Xbox Live Marketplace. Hot on the heels of the 360 demo release, PlayStation 3 owners will be able to get the same demo on their own hardware on October 11th via the PlayStation Network Store.

citation_scope.reason: The passage states a factual event about the release of game demos, which is directly attributable to the cited source. There is no indication that the citation is used for methods, concepts, or related work.

arbiter.missing_subclaim: Nihilistic made demos of the game available (the source attributes the announcement to THQ and mentions a single demo, not multiple demos)
arbiter.rewrite_suggestion: After the game was released in Europe, a playable demo of the game was made available for downloading over Xbox Live and PlayStation Network.
proofs: THQ today announced that a new playable demo of its upcoming action title Conan has gone live on the Xbox Live Marketplace.
Hot on the heels of the 360 demo release, PlayStation 3 owners will be able to get the same demo on their own hardware on October 11th via the PlayStation Network Store.
Release Date: September 28, 2007 (UK)
Release Date: September 27, 2007 (AU)
arbiter.why: The shown sentence only proves the Xbox Live component. The source contains evidence for the European release date and PlayStation Network availability, but those are not shown. More critically, the source does not state that Nihilistic made the demo available (it says THQ announced) and refers to a single demo, not multiple demos. Therefore the claim cannot be supported as written; the author must either add a citation for the unsupported subclaim or rewrite to remove the unattributed actor and plural.
